code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test07-APR-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and mild ketonuria, with abnormal urine appearance and color. These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another form of renal dysfunction. No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment of glycemic control are recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
